# File locks are ad hoc, Tier-1 (attribution only, no auth) — exercise
# acquire/list/release and the exclusive-conflict rule end-to-end.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state
mkdir target

exec breeze lock acquire target/file.txt --as alice --ttl 5m --json
cp stdout lock.json
grep '"id"' lock.json

exec breeze lock list --json
stdout '"holder":\s*"alice"'

# A second exclusive acquire on the same path must fail while the first is held.
! exec breeze lock acquire target/file.txt --as bob --ttl 5m
stderr 'conflict|lock'

# `lock check` is read-only: it never acquires anything, it just reports whether
# someone ELSE holds a conflicting lock — built for gating an external action (e.g. a
# PreToolUse hook) on "is this safe to touch" without also managing a release.
! exec breeze lock check target/file.txt --as bob
stdout 'locked:.*held by alice'
exec breeze lock check target/file.txt --as alice
stdout 'clear'

exec breeze stop
